persona_name: Marcus — Returning User with Multiple Trips

bugs.title: GetChatHistory returns 0 messages for session with known messages
bugs.description: After sending 2 messages in session 8426b424-5d09-4ea9-8d9d-922c4680f918 (selection + planning for Greece trip), GetChatHistory returned an empty messages array. ListChatSessions correctly showed the session exists. This suggests messages may not be persisting to Firestore or there is a retrieval issue.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Send messages in CHAT_MODE_SELECTION and CHAT_MODE_PLANNING. 2. Call GetChatHistory with the session_id and trip_id.
bugs.expected: Messages array with at least 2 user messages and 2 assistant messages

bugs.title: Usage counter only counts 2 of 4 AI messages sent
bugs.description: Sent 4 messages total via SendMessage (1 selection for Greece, 1 planning for Greece, 1 companion for Morocco, 1 selection for NZ). Usage endpoint reports used=2. Selection mode messages may intentionally not count, but companion mode should count. If selection mode is excluded by design, the count should be 2 (planning + companion), which matches. However, if all AI messages should count, this is undercounting.
bugs.expected: Usage count of 4 (all messages) or 2 (if selection mode excluded by design)

ux_issues.description: When asking for a 3-day Santorini itinerary, the AI called create_itinerary_items 3 times — first call succeeded with 9 items, 2nd and 3rd calls detected duplicates and returned 'already_exists'. This caused the final response text to have duplicated summary paragraphs at the end. The dedup logic works correctly (no duplicate items created), but the AI should not re-attempt the same tool call after receiving an 'already_exists' response.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider adding a stronger instruction in the system prompt telling the AI not to retry create_itinerary_items after receiving an already_exists status, or cap tool loop iterations for this specific pattern.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI called create_itinerary_items 3 times with identical items in a single turn. The first call succeeded (9 items created), but the AI then called it 2 more times with the same items in subsequent tool loop iterations, receiving 'already_exists' each time.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Step 5: Planning mode Santorini itinerary request

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI did not call create_itinerary_items for the New Zealand trip overview, only providing a text outline. While the user didn't explicitly ask for itinerary items, the system prompt instructs proactive itinerary creation when discussing plans.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Step 8: New trip creation for NZ — AI gave a 14-day text outline but did not add items to the itinerary

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The core multi-trip workflow works excellently. Vague reference matching ('my Greece trip' -> 'Greek Islands Hopping') worked perfectly on first try. Trip switching ('take me to my Morocco trip') correctly selected the existing trip. New trip creation for NZ correctly created a new trip rather than matching existing ones. The Santorini 3-day itinerary was detailed and well-structured with 9 high-quality items across 3 days. The Morocco companion mode response was contextually appropriate. The main issues are cosmetic: duplicate tool calls causing response text duplication, and missing itinerary items for the NZ overview.
